title: Week-4 Eval Dashboard

provenance.dataset_version_note: 40-case golden; 16 happy / 9 edge / 5 known_failure / 10 adversarial; splits seed/dev/negative_control, no frozen test split
provenance.redaction_policy: Committed dashboard data contains aggregate metrics, synthetic case IDs, and public-safe caveats only; no raw learner text, tutor prose, retrieved spans, raw traces, private URLs, secrets, or frozen test data.

hero.primary_question: Did the Week-4 improvements measurably help without breaking grounded refusal safety?
hero.verdict: Citation F1 improved by +0.150 and turn p95 latency improved by 27%; retrieval recall and refusal recall held at 1.000. Tradeoffs: refusal precision fell from 0.833 to 0.791 and task completion moved from 94.7% infra-excluded baseline to 93.3% current mean; citation F1 remains about 0.31 below the 0.90 plan pass bar.

kpis.label: Citation F1
kpis.note: Improved materially, but still below the 0.90 plan pass bar.

kpis.label: Turn p95 latency
kpis.note: The clearest speed win from loop controls and attribution work.

kpis.label: Refusal recall
kpis.note: The never-bluff guardrail stayed intact across all three runs.

kpis.label: Retrieval recall@5
kpis.note: Retrieval stayed healthy; failures are mostly post-retrieval behavior.

kpis.label: Task completion
kpis.note: Baseline excludes two infra errors; current mean includes all 40 cases.

kpis.label: Refusal precision
kpis.note: Small-N precision moved with a few teachable false refusals.

metric_deltas.note: Baseline excluded two infra errors; current runs had no infra exclusions.
Remaining misses are concentrated false refusals.
Improved, but below the 0.90 plan pass bar.
Tiny-N metric; a few false refusals move it visibly.
The core grounding safety guardrail held.
Slight movement; not the main story.
Retrieval was not the primary remaining bottleneck.
Token usage stayed in the same band.
Baseline and run 1 pricing env vars were unset; use runs 2 and 3 only as current cost reference.

guardrails.label: Refusal recall
guardrails.note: All refusal-expected cases still refused instead of bluffing.

guardrails.label: Retrieval recall@5
guardrails.note: Expected spans remained retrievable in the top five.

guardrails.label: Scorer integrity

remaining_failures.summary: Stable false refusal across all three current-main runs.
Stable false refusal; do not lower STOP threshold casually.
False refusal in two of three current-main runs.
No current infra failure, but kept on watchlist from historical instability.

perspectives.user: The tutor is faster and cites better, and it still refuses unsupported questions. The visible product risk is over-refusing a few teachable cases.
perspectives.builder: Retrieval is healthy; remaining work is mostly post-retrieval decision behavior, false-refusal calibration, and citation quality rather than index recall.

notes: Current values are the mean of three full current-main runs.
Citation improved materially but remains below the original aspirational pass bar.
Cost delta vs baseline is not meaningful because baseline and run 1 pricing env vars were unset.
Case IDs are synthetic scenario labels and carry no learner text or corpus text.
Private project and dataset URLs belong in local-only notes or submission materials, not in this committed dashboard.
